problem_statement: The current centre is visually empty and structurally weak.
Side panels read as detached cards.
The page reads as multiple interface blocks rather than one show Arena.

decisions.decision: Use one edge-to-edge broadcast shell with the Arena floor occupying the primary visual layer.
decisions.reason: The floor must carry the visual hierarchy; information panels frame it rather than compete with it.

decisions.decision: Place challenger-left, Crown Holder centre, and opponent-right inside one confrontation band over the floor.
decisions.reason: This removes the dead centre and gives live data an intentional future binding location.

decisions.decision: Keep the complete eight-rank progression centred over the far half of the floor at desktop, tablet, and mobile.
decisions.reason: Rank progression is Arena structure, not optional desktop decoration.

decisions.decision: Use paired vertical interface rails with connector frames, shared materials, and matched rhythm.
decisions.reason: Status, metrics, ladder, and gifts should feel attached to the stage architecture.

decisions.decision: Use one lower rail for status, ticker, and calls to action.
decisions.reason: The bottom edge completes the broadcast frame and avoids isolated floating controls.

decisions.decision: Use static CSS perspective, rotateX, gradients, shadows, and layered HTML.
decisions.reason: These reproduce the approved cinematic 2D composition without a true 3D engine or interactive camera.

decisions.decision: Represent hall energy only with abstract aria-hidden light texture.
decisions.reason: Atmospheric depth is allowed, but synthetic people, seats, identities, and user impersonation are prohibited.

decisions.decision: Use labels such as Participant binding, Rank position, and Activity position.
decisions.reason: The prototype demonstrates structure without inventing chefs, viewers, flags, or production data.

token_strategy.rule: All raw color values are centralized as named prototype Arena tokens in :root; implementation rules consume variables only.
token_strategy.production_note: Prototype token names are design evidence; official production token adoption requires a separate integration task.

responsive_behavior.desktop_1280x720: Viewport-filling show shell; paired side rails overlap the stage edge; floor remains dominant; lower broadcast rail remains visible.
responsive_behavior.tablet_768: Core scene remains first; full confrontation and rank structures remain on the floor; side rails become a paired two-column support band.
responsive_behavior.mobile_390: Core scene remains first; confrontation compresses to three columns; all eight ranks remain visible; side rails stack below without hiding essential structure; lower actions remain available.
responsive_behavior.horizontal_overflow_rule: The isolated prototype must have no document-level horizontal overflow at any target width.

accessibility: Semantic header, navigation, main, sections, asides, ordered lists, description lists, and footer.
Neutral decorative atmosphere and floor layers are aria-hidden.
No essential structure is hidden at mobile.
Reduced-motion media query disables non-essential transition and animation duration.
non_goals: No real battle or demo data.
No backend, model, template, CSS, JavaScript, or test modification outside the prototype directory.
